Identify stakeholders using org charts.source_update: Mars contacts confirmed from W-01/W-02/W-03 session transcripts, 2026-03-31

practitioner_doer.data_in_snowflake: TBD — dataset structure (tokenised IDs, no raw PII, structured naming) is consistent with warehouse landing layer. Source system TBD from client.
practitioner_doer.prior_modeling: No evidence — no model_score, prev_risk_flag, or campaign_treatment column. Current state is likely rule-based RFM (recency threshold). No ML baseline exists.
data_quality_issues: No nulls in sample — but sample is curated (80 rows); real data quality TBD
Churn definition implicit — churn_label=1 aligns exactly with days_since_last_purchase >= 90. Must be formally agreed as label definition before training.
Bronze tier 60.7% churn rate (17/28) — unusually high, needs investigation for label contamination or sampling bias in production
All 11 RC_Direct churned customers have vet_referred=Y — prescription lapse likely real failure mode, needs validation on larger population
email_open_rate for all churned customers rounds to 0.0 — risk of post-churn data leakage; must check during EDA

practitioner_leader.scoring_cadence: Weekly batch — confirmed. Feature windows are 90d and 12m aggregates; days_since_last_purchase distribution shows risk building over weeks. Daily adds no signal for this feature set.
out_of_scope_phase1: Real-time event scoring — no event timestamps or clickstream in data
Uplift/causal modeling — no treatment/control assignment column
Multi-touch attribution — no campaign sequence data
Geographic/demographic segmentation beyond region/city — no age, household, income data

business_user.primary_goal: Protect RC_Direct revenue by reducing churn from 31.4%. RC_Direct active customers average INR 61,202/year vs PED_Club INR 17,719/year — RC_Direct is simultaneously highest value and highest churn risk.
business_user.segment_focus: Both RC_Direct and PED_Club in scope. RC_Direct is clear priority — 3.4x revenue per active customer, higher churn rate, distinct vet-referral signal.
business_user.current_process: Implied rule-based recency engine — days_since_last_purchase is the only time-based signal and perfectly predicts label. No prev_risk_flag or treatment history. Likely manual trigger: no purchase in 90 days = at risk. Model must identify risk BEFORE day 90 using declining engagement signals.
business_user.pii_restrictions: PII already handled in sample — tokenised customer_id (MARS-IN-XXXXXX), no names/emails/phones. Pseudonymisation policy likely in place. Formal DPA and field-level classification TBD from Mars Legal.

business_leader.success_6m: Reduce overall churn from 27.5% to below 18% in scored population. RC_Direct specifically: 31.4% to below 20%. Target Bronze tier (60.7% churn) and Silver tier (18.5%) for intervention. Gold and Platinum have 0% churn — no retention spend needed.
business_leader.cost_of_inaction: 11 churned RC_Direct customers × INR 61,202 avg active annual spend = ~INR 6.7L annualised at-risk revenue in 80-customer sample. Scales linearly. 100% of churned customers have email_open_rate ~0 and whatsapp_engaged=0 — no win-back channel exists once churned. Churn is effectively one-way.

Prepare structured questionnaires coveri.data_enrichment_note: Data was used to validate and enrich user answers — NOT to substitute for them. User answers are the authoritative source.

technical_constraints: Snowflake tier: TBD — data appears pre-aggregated upstream (90d/12m windows already computed). Read/write pattern needs confirmation before pipeline design.
Python version: TBD — default 3.10+, but MWAA pins to its managed version. Must confirm with Mars IT.
Library restrictions: TBD — enterprise CPG clients often have InfoSec-approved package lists. Confirm with Mars IT.
Data volume: Sample is 80 rows; real population likely hundreds of thousands. Snowflake query cost for 90d/12m feature windows at scale needs testing.

ml_framework.primary: LightGBM — handles Bronze-tier class imbalance (60.7% churn), calibrated probabilities for threshold scoring
ml_framework.baseline: Logistic Regression — interpretable, fast to validate against rule-based current state
ml_framework.note: Mu Sigma recommendation — open to override if Mars has mandate (e.g. existing SageMaker/Azure ML environment)

top_3_phase1_deliverables: 1. Agreed churn label definition ratified by Mars — churn_label maps to days_since_last_purchase >= 90 implicitly. Must be formally agreed before any training. If definition changes, feature windows and pipeline rework follow.
2. Production-ready weekly scoring DAG in Airflow — not a notebook, not a manual script. Break the manual/rule-based dependency.
3. Scored output table consumable by Mars CRM/campaign team without DS involvement — agreed output schema, decision threshold, handoff process. Not just model accuracy.
internal_pressures: Harshita leading alongside parallel commitments — full allocation not assumed
Vishwavani and Savikar allocation % not formally locked — resourcing risk if other projects compete

Hard deadline: June 30, 2026 — ~13 weeks from March 31. Tight for: data access → EDA → feature engineering → model training → pipeline build → UAT → production. Any DPA or data access delay eats directly into this window.

top_3_mars_expectations: 1. Early warning on RC_Direct customers BEFORE day 90 — signals at 30–45 days of declining engagement (email_open_rate, whatsapp_engaged, loyalty_points_balance). By day 90 it is already too late.
2. Ranked score list loadable directly into campaign tool — weekly file or Snowflake table: customer_id, churn_score, risk_tier (High/Medium/Low), recommended_action. No DS required to act on it.

3. Bronze-tier prioritisation — Bronze has 60.7% churn rate. Retention budget is finite. Model must surface highest-probability churners. Gold and Platinum have 0% churn — targeting them wastes spend.
de_ds_tensions: Feature leakage risk: subscription_active=N and days_since_subscription_change are perfectly correlated with churn. Must resolve before training: is subscription cancellation a cause of churn (valid feature) or synonymous with churn (leakage)? If it is the churn event itself, it cannot be a predictor.

Aggregation ownership: 90d/12m windows are pre-computed in data. DE will own aggregation in Snowflake SQL. DS may want different windows during experimentation. Feature store ownership and freeze point must be agreed before parallel work starts — otherwise DE builds one thing and DS trains on another.

business_leader.single_most_important_outcome: Reduce RC_Direct churn from 31.4% to below 20% within two scored quarters post-launch. RC_Direct = INR 61,202/year vs PED_Club INR 17,719 — 3.4x more valuable. Pipeline ROI lives or dies on RC_Direct retention.

out_of_scope_phase1.Real-time scoring: No event stream data — all features are batch aggregates
out_of_scope_phase1.Automated model retraining: Requires monitoring infrastructure not in scope yet
out_of_scope_phase1.CRM integration/campaign execution: Output table is Phase 1; CRM integration is Phase 2
out_of_scope_phase1.Recommendation engine: No historical treatment/response data — cannot train
out_of_scope_phase1.LTV modelling: No pricing, margin, or CLV columns in data
out_of_scope_phase1.Churn reason analysis: num_complaints_12m only qualitative signal — insufficient for root-cause modelling

Capture and consolidate stakeholder expe.data_enrichment: mars_churn_sample.csv used to validate and quantify answers only

W01.attendee_mars: Priya Sharma — Head of CRM & Retention, Mars Petcare India

current_process.trigger: Recency threshold — 45 days for RC Direct, ~60 days for Pedigree Club
current_process.delivery: SQL query (Redash) emails Excel CSV every Monday morning
current_process.run_by: Karthik team schedules via cron job — fragile, missed 2 weeks ago due to server maintenance
current_process.manual_processing: ~2 hours every Monday by coordinator Deepa — filtering by segment, removing recently contacted, removing customers with pending delivery, rough prioritisation by spend
current_process.single_point_of_failure: Deepa — only person who knows full filter logic. Undocumented.
current_process.churn_definition: NOT formally defined. Internally: lapsed=45d, churned=90d. Marketing says 60d. Finance says 120d. Inconsistent across teams.

retention_actions.RC_Direct_sequence: Day 45: WhatsApp (personalised with vet name) → Day 50: Email → Day 55: Outbound call from 7-person retention desk
retention_actions.RC_Direct_capacity: 4,500–5,000 outbound calls per week. Normal at-risk volume: 1,200–1,500/week. Peak: ~3,000/week. Capacity not a constraint for RC Direct.
retention_actions.Pedigree_Club: Email and WhatsApp only — no calls unless Gold tier or >INR 20K annual spend. 8x customer base but no call capacity.
retention_actions.whatsapp_tool: Kaleyra via WABA. NOT integrated with SFMC — two separate tools, manual reconciliation.
retention_actions.retention_tracking: Re-purchase within 30 days of contact = retention save. NO holdout group. Reported rates likely inflated.
retention_actions.current_retention_rate: ~22% RC Direct, ~14% Pedigree Club (self-reported, no holdout)

pain_points: Find out too late — wants signals at 20–25 days, not 45+
Ghost purchasers — low-baseline customers never trigger 45-day rule
Channel-switch false positives — customers buying via Amazon/vet clinic flagged as at-risk when they are fine
Two separate campaign tools (SFMC + Kaleyra) require manual reconciliation
No individual-level engagement trending — only aggregate open rates

output_requirements.landing_zone: Salesforce — not just Snowflake. CRM team needs it importable into SFMC without IT involvement.
output_requirements.scoring_deadline: Scores in Salesforce by Monday EOD. Campaign brief to SFMC by Tuesday 6 PM for Wednesday send. Sunday night scoring preferred.
output_requirements.risk_tiers: 4 tiers required: High / Medium / Low / Do Not Contact (suppression for Gold+Platinum)
consumers: Rohan (manager — executive summary dashboard: at-risk count, actioned count, recovery rate)

new_stakeholders_identified: Priya Sharma — Head of CRM & Retention (Mars)
Deepa — Retention coordinator (Mars) — key UAT participant
Rohan — Priya's manager (Mars) — executive dashboard consumer
confirmed_decisions: Output: 4-tier label (High / Medium / Low / Do Not Contact)
open_items: Formal churn definition — needs cross-functional alignment (CRM + Finance + Analytics)
Salesforce integration spec — Phase 1 out vs in scope
Deepa to be included in UAT

W02.attendee_mars: Karthik Nair — Analytics & Data Lead, Mars India

data_architecture.Salesforce_CRM: Customer master in Snowflake via Fivetran
data_architecture.Oracle_ecommerce: Transaction DB in Snowflake via Fivetran
data_architecture.Capillary_loyalty: SFTP daily dump → S3 → custom Python script → Snowflake. Fragile — format changed twice in last year. No data quality checks.
data_architecture.dbt_marts: dbt project with  schema. 90d and 12m windows recomputed daily. Full refresh model — ~90 min runtime on medium warehouse.
data_architecture.complaint_data: Freshdesk. num_complaints_12m in sample was manual export — production ingestion pipeline not built yet.
data_architecture.other_data: Nielsen panel data (aggregate, not individual) — not useful for Phase 1 individual scoring

prior_modeling.model: XGBoost by internal DS Manish — early 2025
prior_modeling.outcome: Classic feature leakage — subscription_active used as feature, raw last_purchase_date used. 90% accuracy on test set, ~15% precision at top decile in production. Never deployed.
prior_modeling.lesson: Subscription cancellation may be synonymous with churn event — must not be used as feature
prior_modeling.notebooks: Available — Karthik to share after session

data_quality.Fivetran_freshness: Runs nightly at 2 AM. Sunday night scores = transactions up to Friday. 1-2 day lag acceptable for weekly scoring.
data_quality.whatsapp_reliability: Directionally correct but ~90% webhook reliability — not precise
data_quality.identity_bridge: CUST_MASTER.ID_BRIDGE — ~68% coverage RC Direct, ~55% Pedigree Club. ~1/3 of customers have incomplete cross-system identity.

confirmed_decisions: Feature aggregations from existing dbt mart, refactored to incremental
subscription_active: leakage confirmed — must resolve before training
open_items: Karthik to share prior XGBoost notebooks
Decision on subscription_active feature vs label
Freshdesk production ingestion pipeline — DE scope
Identity bridge coverage flag in model output

W03.attendee_mars: Rahul Mehta — IT Platform & Infrastructure Lead, Mars India

infrastructure.Snowflake_accounts: Separate non-prod and prod accounts. Dev=MARS_DEV schema, Staging=MARS_STG schema (within non-prod). MARS_PROD = separate account.
infrastructure.PII_in_nonprod: Tokenised customer IDs, masked email/phone. Same as sample data.
infrastructure.Mu_Sigma_access: Service account for pipelines. Write access to MU_SIGMA_DEV schema in MARS_DEV.
infrastructure.warehouse_sizing: Start on X-Small. Scale-up via change request — no auto-provisioning to vendors.

airflow.deployment_access: Karthik team has dev DAG access. Production requires ServiceNow change request + architecture board + 1-week UAT = 2–4 weeks total.

clauses_under_review: Sub-processor clause (resolved: all processing in Mars Snowflake — no external infra)
DPA.estimated_completion: 3–4 weeks. Data access earliest early May. Safe assumption: first week of May.
DPA.implication: April = design and specification only. No pipeline code against live data until DPA signed.

constraints.vet_referred_field: Under InfoSec health data classification review. Treat as optional feature — do NOT make load-bearing. Ruling expected in 2–3 weeks.
constraints.production_freeze: Oct 1 – Nov 15 annually. No production changes in this window.
constraints.production_deployment_cycle: 2–4 weeks from code complete. Must reach code-complete by ~June 1 for June 30 deadline.
constraints.service_account_provisioning: 2-week SLA — flag early after DPA signed.

confirmed_decisions: All training and inference stays within Mars Snowflake — no data leaves to external infrastructure
Snowflake: X-Small for dev, scale-up request for production
DPA-first policy — no data access until signed
open_items: LightGBM package request — submit as soon as DPA signed
Service account provisioning — flag early (2-week SLA)

practitioner_doer.note: Document full as-is including Karthik data layer, not just Priya business process

practitioner_leader.scope: Full as-is — both business/CRM process (Priya) AND data pipeline process (Karthik Redash + dbt)

business_leader.note: Weekly retention summary to Rohan is part of as-is reporting workflow

practitioner_doer.manual_effort: Deepa: ~2 hrs every Monday = ~104 hrs/year on list processing

build_run_cost.assumptions: Standard India consulting rates; Infra = Snowflake X-Small + MWAA small. Pending confirmation from Mu Sigma Finance and Rahul/IT on actual AWS bill.

practitioner_leader.analyst_time_current: ~104 hrs/year (Deepa manual list processing) + Karthik team cron maintenance

cost_per_outreach_attempt.RC_Direct_full_sequence: INR 13.10 per customer per cycle (WhatsApp + Email + Call)
cost_per_outreach_attempt.note: Estimates — Mars Finance to confirm Kaleyra contract rate and fully-loaded call centre cost

revenue_impact_10pp_reduction.mars_preapproved_target: Not confirmed — Mars Finance to validate INR 61,202 avg spend on full 285K population and approved churn target

risk_quantification.SPOF_Deepa: One person holds all filter logic — any absence halts weekly campaign execution
risk_quantification.Capillary_fragility: Format changed twice in last year — each incident = data gap in campaign targeting
risk_quantification.no_holdout: Reported retention rates (22% RC Direct) are inflated — true baseline unknown
risk_quantification.DPA_delay: 4-week DPA delay = 4 weeks of avoidable churn during design-only period
risk_quantification.vet_referred_classification: InfoSec ruling pending — may remove a key signal from RC Direct model

Quantify business impact (time, cost, ri.data_source: User-provided answers (Harshita Gupta) from W-01/W-02/W-03 sessions + sample data enrichment. Mars Finance validation pending on cost figures.

practitioner_doer.core_technical_problem: Binary classification: predict which individual customers will churn (lapse > 90 days) within the next 30 days, using 90d/12m behavioural aggregates, loyalty signals, and engagement metrics — scored weekly at customer level, output to Snowflake + Salesforce.
predictive_signals: days_since_last_purchase — primary recency signal (current rule is this alone at 45d threshold)
email_open_rate_90d and whatsapp_engaged_90d — engagement decay preceding churn
loyalty_points_balance — declining balance signals reduced brand attachment
loyalty_tier (Bronze highest risk at 60.7% churn rate)
vet_referred (ALL 11 churned RC Direct customers in sample — if InfoSec clears)
subscription_active — LEAKAGE RISK: must resolve whether this is a cause or synonymous with churn label before use
excluded_features: raw last_purchase_date (use delta/recency, not raw date)

practitioner_leader.one_sentence_problem: Build a weekly ML-driven churn prediction pipeline that identifies RC Direct and Pedigree Club customers at risk of lapsing before the current 45-day rule triggers, enabling Mars India to run targeted retention campaigns and reduce RC Direct churn from 31.4% to below 20%.

success_target.primary: RC Direct churn rate: 31.4% → below 20% within 2 scored quarters post-launch
success_target.secondary: Pedigree Club churn rate reduction (14% retention → 20%+ retention)
success_target.operational: Eliminate Deepa SPOF — scored list auto-generated in Snowflake/Salesforce by Sunday night, no manual intervention
success_target.model: Precision at top decile > 40% (vs 15% in prior XGBoost attempt)

business_user.trigger: Two converging failures: (1) Capillary SFTP broke 2 weeks ago — at-risk list was not generated for a full week, customers churned untouched. (2) Deepa is the only person who knows the filter logic — one absence halts the entire retention program. The current system is too fragile for a 285K-customer program.
current_process_breakdown: Rule fires too late: 45-day recency threshold means customer has already disengaged for 6+ weeks before first outreach
Ghost purchasers never trigger: low-frequency customers with longer natural purchase cycles always look at-risk
Channel-switch false positives: customers buying via Amazon or vet clinic flagged as churning when they are not
Manual reconciliation: SFMC and Kaleyra are two separate tools — Deepa manually reconciles every Monday, ~2hrs
No holdout: reported 22% retention rate is inflated — no control group, no true baseline

business_leader.decision_enabled: Who to call, email, and WhatsApp this week — and who NOT to contact (Gold/Platinum suppression, channel fatigue avoidance). Today this is a manual judgement call by Deepa. The pipeline replaces that with a ranked, risk-tiered scored list: High / Medium / Low / Do Not Contact — ready in Salesforce by Monday morning.
business_leader.consequence_of_inaction: At current RC Direct churn rate of 31.4%: ~89,550 RC Direct customers churn annually. At INR 61,202 avg spend, that is ~INR 548 Cr gross revenue lost per year. The retention program is operating blind — no model, no holdout, no signal before day 45. Every week without this pipeline, ~1,700 RC Direct customers cross the 45-day threshold having received no early signal-based outreach.

Define clear problem statement aligned w.data_source: Synthesised from Issues #1–6 confirmed answers. No new user input required.
